title: platform-user — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `IsPlatformUser` is a marker composed by the PlatformUser model itself for reflection-based tooling (audit correlator, on-call resolver, offboarding coordinator). `HasEmploymentLifecycle` composes the state-machine transitions (hire → activate → suspend? → offboard) with the observers that gate them + fire the matching events. Critically, NO `BelongsToTenant` — platform_users is cross-plane by construction.

owned.description: Marker trait composed by the PlatformUser model. Provides the shared shape identifier for reflection-based tooling that must enumerate every PlatformUser-shaped row without hard-coding the class (audit correlator, on-call rotation service, offboarding coordinator). Adds `->isPlatformStaff()` boolean check + `->guardName()` returning `'platform_admin'` (used by Sanctum + spatie-permission to disambiguate from the `sanctum` guard). Adds no accessor / mutator / scope of its own — those live on HasEmploymentLifecycle.

Composable state-machine transitions for the employment lifecycle. Ships: `->activate()` (from pending → active), `->suspend(string $reason)` (from active → suspended — temporary), `->restore()` (from suspended → active), `->offboard(string $reason, ?Carbon $effective_at = null)` (any → offboarded, terminal), `->reassignManager(?PlatformUser $newManager)`. Each transition wraps in a DB transaction, fires the matching event, writes to the audit trail via HasActivityLog, and refuses invalid transitions (e.g. `offboard()` on an already-offboarded row is a no-op; `activate()` on an offboarded row throws `EmploymentLifecycleTransitionRefused`). Every transition captures the actor id (the PlatformUser invoking the action, resolved from the request context) so the audit trail attributes the action correctly.
owned.wraps: State machine is hand-rolled (no `spatie/laravel-model-states` dependency) because the transitions are simple enough to inline and the observer already gates them. Ordering matters — `HasEmploymentLifecycle` MUST be composed AFTER `IsPlatformUser` so the marker trait's guard-name resolver is available to the lifecycle transitions (which stamp audit rows with the guard name).

framework.reason: PlatformUser retains a 90-day post-offboarding hold (config('platform-user.retention.hold_days')) before hard delete so audit rows keep resolvable actor names during the compliance retrieval window.
Consumes the same notification channels as tenant Users (mail, in-app) plus platform-specific channels (Slack via the internal-notifications transport, PagerDuty webhook via the on-call transport).
Standard Sanctum PAT storage on the `platform_admin` guard. Same abstraction as tenant Users; the guard name is the distinction.

notes: NO `BelongsToTenant` — PlatformUser is cross-plane (design.md §4.3, tenancy-columns.md §2 exception). Any refactor that adds `BelongsToTenant` here is a compliance violation and the tenancy-compliance-auditor agent will refuse it.
NO application-scope trait — PlatformUser spans every Application. Platform staff can query cross-application data by definition.
`IsPlatformUser` MUST precede `HasEmploymentLifecycle` so the marker trait's guard-name resolver is available to lifecycle observer wiring.

`HasAudit` is composed HEAVIER on PlatformUser than on tenant User — every column mutation is audit-logged with old + new values (even non-restricted-tier fields) because SOC2 CC7.2 requires the fuller trail for staff plane.
PII cluster — phone_e164, avatar_url, slack_handle, github_handle. Redactor rules apply unless the caller carries `platform.platform_profiles.view.pii`.
SoftDeletes cascades from PlatformUser (deleted_at set at the same moment; hard delete via PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob deletes both rows in one transaction).
